titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Malachy J. Kelly to James Brown, 23 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Malachy J. Kelly

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter is from Malachy J. Kelly (1851-1916), Chief Crown Solicitor, to James Brown. It relates to the death and legacy of Elizabeth Hogg and requests a statement from Dr. McIvor.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3888

correspAction.placeName: Chief Secretary's Office, Dublin Castle, Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
DUBLIN CASTLE 23rd March 1916
Elizabeth Hogg decd. Dear Sir, I submitted your letter and copy affidavit of John Duff
to the Attorney General for his directions. It appears by the affidavit that Dr McIvor was present when
the alleged delivery of the Deposit Receipt was made to Mr John
Duff. As the validity of a donatio mortis causa depends upon what exactly took place at the time of the gift — & the circumstances under which it was made, The physical & mental condition of the donor & whether she contemplated immediate death The Attorney General would be glad to receive from Dr McIvor
a statement of what occurred on the occasion and I have written to
him accordingly I should be glad if you could get one from him & send it to me as soon as convenient. Yours truly, James Browne Esq Solicitor, Broad Street,
Magherafelt.
